perturbation.original_text: Unless a particular portion of Proprietary Information becomes nonproprietary as provided in Section 4(a) hereof, or unless otherwise authorized in a written agreement signed by an authorized officer of STI, Pinetree has a duty to protect each particular portion of Proprietary Information in for a period of five (5) years from the date of this Agreement, provided that Proprietary Information which constitutes a trade secret under applicable law shall be protected as long as the Proprietary Information qualifies as a trade secret under Maryland law.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless a particular portion of Proprietary Information becomes nonproprietary as provided in Section 4(a) hereof, or unless otherwise authorized in a written agreement signed by an authorized officer of STI, Pinetree has a duty to protect each particular portion of Company Information in for a period of five (5) years from the date of this Agreement, provided that Proprietary Information which constitutes a trade secret under applicable law shall be protected as long as the Proprietary Information qualifies as a trade secret under Maryland law.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Proprietary Information" to "Company Information" in Section 2 but leaving "Proprietary Information" elsewhere introduces ambiguity. It is unclear if Pinetree's duty extends to all "Proprietary Information" or just "Company Information" as defined here. It contradicts section 1 which states "Proprietary Information" and creates confusion on which definition to follow.
perturbation.contradicted_text: DEFINITION OF PROPRIETARY INFORMATION. As used in this Agreement, the term "Proprietary Information" means any confidential, non-public or proprietary information, technology or documentation, including without limitation, drawings, designs, business plans and proposals, ideas and prototypes that may be disclosed by STI to Pinetree, whether such is of a technical, commercial, organizational, scientific, financial or business nature.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Proprietary Information' with 'Company Information' in Section 2 while the contradicted text in Section 1 defines 'Proprietary Information'. This creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether Pinetree's duty to protect applies to all 'Proprietary Information' as defined in Section 1, or only to 'Company Information' as defined in Section 2. This misalignment of terminology leads to a strong in-text contradiction and ambiguity, fitting the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: Pinetree must protect the Proprietary Information from unauthorized use or disclosure by exercising the same degree of care that Pinetree uses with respect to information of its own of a similar, highly valuable nature, and Pinetree must, at minimum, use at least reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: Pinetree must protect the Sensitive Information from unauthorized use or disclosure by exercising the same degree of care that Pinetree uses with respect to information of its own of a similar, highly valuable nature, and Pinetree must, at minimum, use at least reasonable care.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Proprietary Information" to "Sensitive Information" in Section 3(b) creates an in-text contradiction, because Proprietary Information is reffered in sections 3 a. and 3 c.. It becomes unclear whether the protection standard applies to "Sensitive Information" or the original "Proprietary Information" as used elsewhere, especially in regards to duplication as per 3. c.
perturbation.contradicted_text: DUPLICATION. Pinetree shall not copy, duplicate, replicate, decompile or reverse engineer in any manner whatsoever (whether physically, electronically, in writing or otherwise), in whole or in part, any part of the Proprietary Information without the prior written consent of STI, which consent shall be in STI's sole and absolute discretion, and will immediately notify STI in writing of any unauthorized disclosure of the Proprietary Information. At any time upon STI's request, Pinetree shall promptly redeliver to STI, or destroy at STI's direction, all written material in any media or format whatsoever containing, reflecting or in any way derived from the Proprietary Information (including notes, summaries, copies, extracts or other reproductions, in whole or in part), regardless of whether the material was prepared by STI or on STI's behalf. The redelivery or destruction of the Proprietary Information shall be certified by Pinetree in writing to STI, and shall not in any way relieve Pinetree of its obligation of confidentiality.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Proprietary Information" to "Sensitive Information" in Section 3(b) introduces an in-text contradiction. Section 3(c), the contradicted text, explicitly refers to "Proprietary Information" and restricts its duplication. The change makes it unclear whether the restrictions in 3(c) apply to "Sensitive Information", "Proprietary Information", or both. This misalignment of terminology, as per the definition provided, creates a contradiction regarding the scope of duplication restrictions and confidentiality obligations, therefore leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: GENERAL EXCEPTIONS. The disclosure and use restrictions imposed in this Agreement do not apply to Proprietary Material that: (a) Pinetree can show by documentary evidence was already in Pinetree's possession before the disclosure hereunder to Pinetree; (b) is hereafter disclosed to Pinetree by a third party who has no duty of confidentiality to STI in respect of it; or (c) is or becomes generally available to the public through no act or default on Pinetree's part. Proprietary Material that comprises a combination of features shall not be within any of the exceptions set forth above merely because individual features are known or received by Pinetree or are in or fall into the public domain, but only if the combination is know or received by Pinetree or is in or falls into the public domain.
perturbation.changed_text: GENERAL EXCEPTIONS. The disclosure and use restrictions imposed in this Agreement do not apply to Company Data that: (a) Pinetree can show by documentary evidence was already in Pinetree's possession before the disclosure hereunder to Pinetree; (b) is hereafter disclosed to Pinetree by a third party who has no duty of confidentiality to STI in respect of it; or (c) is or becomes generally available to the public through no act or default on Pinetree's part. Proprietary Material that comprises a combination of features shall not be within any of the exceptions set forth above merely because individual features are known or received by Pinetree or are in or fall into the public domain, but only if the combination is know or received by Pinetree or is in or falls into the public domain.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Proprietary Material” to “Company Data” only in the first sentence of Section 4(a) while retaining “Proprietary Material” in the rest of the section introduces contradiction. This creates uncertainty as to whether the exception applies to all Proprietary Material, or just the subset now defined as Company Data. The original definition in section 1 reffered to Proprietary Information, so it is not clear if this exception applies to that too.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EXCEPTIONS. a. GENERAL EXCEPTIONS. The disclosure and use restrictions imposed in this Agreement do not apply to Company Data that: (a) Pinetree can show by documentary evidence was already in Pinetree's possession before the disclosure hereunder to Pinetree; (b) is hereafter disclosed to Pinetree by a third party who has no duty of confidentiality to STI in respect of it; or (c) is or becomes generally available to the public through no act or default on Pinetree's part. Proprietary Material that comprises a combination of features shall not be within any of the exceptions set forth above merely because individual features are known or received by Pinetree or are in or fall into the public domain, but only if the combination is know or received by Pinetree or is in or falls into the public domain.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces “Proprietary Material” with “Company Data” in the first sentence, while the rest of the paragraph still refers to “Proprietary Material”. This creates a contradiction and ambiguity, as it becomes unclear whether the exceptions apply to all Proprietary Material, or only to Company Data. As stated in the Perturbation Explanation, this alteration introduces confusion and contradiction and follows the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Pinetree agrees that all Proprietary Material disclosed shall remain the property of STI, and nothing herein shall be construed as the grant of a license or any other right, either directly or indirectly, by implication, estoppel or otherwise, to any Proprietary Material.
perturbation.changed_text: Pinetree agrees that all STI Data disclosed shall remain the property of STI, and nothing herein shall be construed as the grant of a license or any other right, either directly or indirectly, by implication, estoppel or otherwise, to any Proprietary Material.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "Proprietary Material" to "STI Data" in the first part of Section 4(c) while retaining "Proprietary Material" later in the same sentence. Creates an in-text contradiction as the property right is reffered to "STI Data", but the license clause refers to "Proprietary Material". This makes unclear whether the property of STI is restricted to STI data, or the Proprietary Material itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: OWNERSHIP; WARRANTIES. Pinetree agrees that all STI Data disclosed shall remain the property of STI, and nothing herein shall be construed as the grant of a license or any other right, either directly or indirectly, by implication, estoppel or otherwise, to any Proprietary Material. STI has no obligation to disclose any Proprietary Information. STI makes no representations or warranties as to the accuracy or completeness of the Proprietary Material, and shall have no liability to Pinetree or its Representatives resulting from Pinetree's use of the Proprietary Material, except as may be expressly set forth in any subsequent, definitive written agreement between the parties. This Agreement does not create an agency, partnership, joint venture or other like relationship.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces "Proprietary Material" with "STI Data" in the first part of the sentence while keeping "Proprietary Material" later in the same sentence. This causes a contradiction within the document. The original text consistently refers to "Proprietary Material" when discussing property rights and license grants. The changed text creates ambiguity by assigning property rights to "STI Data" but still mentioning "Proprietary Material" in the context of license grants. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which states, "Pinetree agrees that all STI Data disclosed shall remain the property of STI, and nothing herein shall be construed as the grant of a license or any other right... to any Proprietary Material." It is unclear if the license applies to STI Data or Proprietary Material, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: STI may take any necessary action to compel specific performance of this Agreement or to enjoin any violation of this Agreement so that Proprietary Material is not used in any manner to harm or adversely affect STI.
perturbation.changed_text: STI may take any necessary action to compel specific performance of this Agreement or to enjoin any violation of this Agreement so that STI Information is not used in any manner to harm or adversely affect STI.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing “Proprietary Material” with “STI Information” in Section 6, concerning legal enforcement, changes the scope of the agreement in-text as STI is reffered to protecting "STI Information", thus it creates uncertainty whether the legal action applies to the initial definition of "Proprietary Information" on Section 1. Therefore creating conflict between Section 1 and Section 6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: DEFINITION OF PROPRIETARY INFORMATION. As used in this Agreement, the term "Proprietary Information" means any confidential, non-public or proprietary information, technology or documentation, including without limitation, drawings, designs, business plans and proposals, ideas and prototypes that may be disclosed by STI to Pinetree, whether such is of a technical, commercial, organizational, scientific, financial or business nature.
perturbation.justification: The original text protected 'Proprietary Material' while the changed text protects 'STI Information'. The contradicted text defines 'Proprietary Information'. If 'STI Information' is not equivalent to 'Proprietary Information' then there is a contradiction because the legal action now covers a different scope of information than originally defined, creating a conflict between the definition in Section 1 and the enforcement in Section 6.
